> **Red Flag** = 🚨 if ≥ 10 FK tables and zero sum/count rules — not triggered (2 count rules present).

## COVERAGE DETAIL

```
Domain tables (11):  Shipment, ShipmentCommodity, ShipmentParty, ShipmentXml,
                     Piece, SpecialHandling, Customer, CustomsOffice,
                     CustomsRegion, VirtualRouteLeg, ControlledRegulatedGood
Excluded (lookup):   GovtDept (2 non-PK cols)
Excluded (SysConfig): SysConfig

Rules by type:  2× count (wt 3), 2× copy (wt 2), 2× formula (wt 2)
                + 2× early_row_event, 1× row_event, 1× after_flush_row_event (wt 0)
Weighted total: 6 + 4 + 4 = 14

Per-table rule coverage:
  Shipment          ████  all 14 pts (2 count, 2 copy, 2 formula)
  ShipmentCommodity  ░░░  0 pts (early_row_event only — weight 0)
  All others         ░░░  0 pts — ungoverned
```

The project governs one table well and leaves ten ungoverned. This is expected for a Phase 2
implementation focused on a specific use case (CLVS eligibility). Phase 3 work should extend
rules to `ShipmentCommodity`, `Customer`, `ShipmentParty`, and `Piece`.

## INTEGRITY FINDINGS

### Docstring hygiene (-1 each)

🟡 **-1** `logic/logic_discovery/isdc_consume.py:4`  
Implementation note in docstring: `"(Step 1 — EAI row_event bridge: after ShipmentXml insert, publish blob.id to isdc_processed)"` — docstring must contain only verbatim requirement text, nothing about implementation mechanics.  
→ Fix: remove the parenthetical; keep only the original prompt excerpt

🟡 **-1** `logic/logic_discovery/shipment_matching.py:3-13`  
Docstring contains creation instructions (`"Create logic/logic_discovery/shipment_matching.py"`) and a rule-type directive (`"Use Rule.row_event (not early_row_event)"`). Anything added becomes a spec the AI codes to on the next iteration.  
→ Fix: replace with the verbatim Step 2 requirement excerpt (from `docs/requirements/shipment_matching/requirements.md`)

### FK columns with no covering index (-1 each = -9)

SQLite does not auto-create FK indexes. Each unindexed FK forces a full table scan on every
`Rule.count` aggregation, cascade delete, and parent lookup on that relationship.

🟡 **-1** `controlled_regulated_goods.govt_dept_id` — no index  
🟡 **-1** `customs_office.customs_region_id` — no index  
🟡 **-1** `piece.local_shipment_oid_nbr` — no index  
🟡 **-1** `shipment.customs_office_id` — no index  
🟡 **-1** `shipment.sys_config_id` — no index  
🟡 **-1** `shipment_commodity.controlled_regulated_goods_id` — no index  
🟡 **-1** `shipment_party.local_piece_oid_nbr` — no index  
🟡 **-1** `shipment_party.local_shipment_oid_nbr` — no index  
🟡 **-1** `special_handling.oid_nbr` — no index  

→ Fix for each: `CREATE INDEX ix_<table>_<col> ON <table>(<col>);` (can be done in one batch)

### Hall passes granted (no demerits)

✅ `_set_commodity_flags` — `early_row_event` with `session.query(...).filter(...).first()` — pattern: `row-lookup`; FK column assignment cannot use `Rule.formula` (CE rule)  
✅ `_set_customs_office` — `early_row_event` with `filter_by(...).first()` — pattern: `row-lookup`  
✅ `_reasons()` — `session.query(CustomsOffice).get(fk_value)` in formula helper — pattern: `row-lookup`; avoids stale relationship per CE  
✅ `_match_importer` — `row_event` creates child ShipmentParty via lookup — pattern: `row-lookup`; no Rule type covers new-entity creation from lookup  
✅ `_publish_isdc_processed` — `after_flush_row_event` checks `is_processed` and publishes — pattern: `eai-consumer-bridge`
